componentDescription: MultiSelect allows selecting multiple values from a dropdown list. Use for tags, permissions, filters, category assignments, and any field where more than one option can be active simultaneously. Selected values appear as COUNT (number badge) or TEXT (comma-separated tags) in the trigger.
features: Two variants: CONTAINER (bordered trigger) and NO_CONTAINER (minimal)
Two selection tag display types: COUNT (shows number of selected) and TEXT (shows selected labels)
Select All option with customizable label
Action buttons in footer (primaryAction/secondaryAction) for apply/cancel patterns
compositionPattern: Items follow the same SelectMenuGroupType[] shape as SingleSelect. selectedValues is a string[] of currently selected item values. Pass onChange to receive the updated string[] on every selection change.

usageExamples.title: Tag/category multi-select
usageExamples.description: Multi-select for assigning categories to a post.
usageExamples.code:
<MultiSelect
  label="Categories"
  placeholder="Select categories"
  selectedValues={selectedCategories}
  onChange={setSelectedCategories}
  enableSearch
  size={MultiSelectMenuSize.MEDIUM}
  items={[
    {
      items: categories.map(c => ({ label: c.name, value: c.id }))
    }
  ]}
/>

usageExamples.title: Permission selector with select all
usageExamples.description: Multi-select for assigning permissions with a select all option and max limit.
usageExamples.code:
<MultiSelect
  label="Permissions"
  placeholder="Select permissions"
  selectedValues={permissions}
  onChange={setPermissions}
  enableSelectAll
  selectAllText="All permissions"
  maxSelections={10}
  selectionTagType={MultiSelectSelectionTagType.COUNT}
  size={MultiSelectMenuSize.MEDIUM}
  items={[
    {
      groupLabel: 'Read',
      items: readPermissions.map(p => ({ label: p.label, value: p.value }))
    },
    {
      groupLabel: 'Write',
      items: writePermissions.map(p => ({ label: p.label, value: p.value }))
    }
  ]}
/>

usageExamples.title: Filter panel multi-select with action buttons
usageExamples.description: Multi-select where changes are applied via an explicit 'Apply' button instead of immediately.
usageExamples.code:
<MultiSelect
  label="Filter by status"
  placeholder="All statuses"
  selectedValues={pendingFilters}
  onChange={setPendingFilters}
  showActionButtons
  primaryAction={{ label: 'Apply', onClick: handleApplyFilters }}
  secondaryAction={{ label: 'Clear', onClick: handleClearFilters }}
  showClearButton
  onClearAllClick={handleClearAll}
  size={MultiSelectMenuSize.MEDIUM}
  items={[{ items: statuses.map(s => ({ label: s.label, value: s.value })) }]}
/>

label.llmContext: Required. Label displayed above the trigger. Describes what is being selected (e.g., 'Tags', 'Assignees', 'Countries').
label.propDescription: Required. Label for the multi-select field

placeholder.llmContext: Required. Trigger text when no items are selected. Use 'Select ...' format or 'All [entity]' to indicate that no filter is active.
placeholder.propDescription: Required. Trigger text when nothing is selected

selectedValues.llmContext: Required. Array of currently selected value strings. Each string must match a value in your items array. Manage with useState initialized to [] for empty selection.
selectedValues.propDescription: Required. Array of currently selected item values

onChange.llmContext: Required. Callback fired with the complete updated string[] when any selection changes. Replace your selectedValues state with the received array.
onChange.propDescription: Required. Called with full updated selected values array

items.llmContext: Required. Same SelectMenuGroupType[] structure as SingleSelect. Each group has optional groupLabel, items array, and showSeparator. Each item: { label, value, subLabel?, slot1?, disabled? }.
items.propDescription: Required. Grouped array of selectable items

selectionTagType.llmContext: COUNT shows a number badge (e.g., '3 selected') — use when labels are long or space is limited, or when the exact selections aren't important to see at a glance. TEXT shows the labels inline — use when the user needs to see which specific items are selected in the trigger.
selectionTagType.propDescription: How selected items are shown in the trigger

enableSelectAll.llmContext: Set to true to add a 'Select All' option at the top of the list. Use when selecting all items is a valid and common use case (e.g., selecting all permissions for an admin role). Provide selectAllText for a custom label.
enableSelectAll.propDescription: Adds a 'Select All' option to the item list

maxSelections.llmContext: Limits the number of items that can be selected. Once the limit is reached, unselected items are disabled. Use for fields with a hard maximum (e.g., 'Choose up to 5 skills').
maxSelections.propDescription: Maximum number of items that can be selected

showActionButtons.llmContext: Set to true to show Apply/Cancel buttons in the dropdown footer. Use for filter panels where selections should only take effect when explicitly applied, rather than on every change.
showActionButtons.propDescription: Shows action buttons (apply/cancel) in the dropdown footer

showClearButton.llmContext: Set to true to show a 'Clear all' button in the trigger or dropdown. Use for filter fields where clearing all selections should be easily accessible.

enableVirtualization.llmContext: Set to true for lists with 100+ items. Prevents performance degradation by only rendering visible items.
enableVirtualization.propDescription: Enables virtual rendering for large item lists
